commit.message:
Fix Cycle 24 Fulfillment QA test failures: .Single() → .First(), 404 for non-existent shipments, and FulfillmentRequestedHandler idempotency (#343)

* Initial plan

* Fix 3 outstanding Cycle 24 Fulfillment QA test failures

- .Single() → .First() in IntegrationMessagePublishingTests (ConfirmDelivery)
- .Single() → .First() in RecordDeliveryFailureTests (publishing test)
- RecordDeliveryFailureHandler.Before() now loads Shipment via IDocumentSession
  to properly return 404 for non-existent shipments (avoids Wolverine 400 short-circuit)
- 404 test body now includes ShipmentId so FluentValidation passes

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Polish fixes per Principal Architect review: CancellationToken, comment clarity, test body consistency

- Add CancellationToken to Before() async method signature
- Improve explanatory comment for IDocumentSession approach
- Add ShipmentId to 400 test body for consistency with 404 test

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix FulfillmentRequestedHandler idempotency: FetchStreamStateAsync guard before StartStream

The handler was calling StartStream unconditionally, causing
ExistingStreamIdCollisionException when a duplicate FulfillmentRequested
message arrived with the same UUID-v5 derived shipmentId.

Now: FetchStreamStateAsync checks if the stream exists before StartStream.
If it exists, the handler returns early (true no-op, no exception).

Handler signature changed from void to async Task with CancellationToken.

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

Show policy checks as expandable tree items under pull requests (#24)

* Initial plan

* feat: show checks as expandable tree items under PRs instead of in tooltip

- Add PolicyCheck interface with individual check name, status, isBlocking
- Update getChecksStatus to getChecks, returning both aggregate status and individual checks
- Extract check display names from policy configuration settings/type
- Make PR tree items collapsible when they have checks

- Remove checks text from PR tooltip
- Add tests for check tree items
- Update vscode mock to support MarkdownString and TreeItem properties

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: address code review feedback - use notApplicable default and lookup map for status labels

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use correct preview API version for policy evaluations endpoint

The policy evaluations API (/_apis/policy/evaluations) is a preview-only
endpoint requiring api-version=7.1-preview.1. Using api-version=7.1 caused
the request to fail silently, resulting in no checks being returned.

Also filter out disabled/deleted policy evaluations.

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: filter checks to only show build/status policy types

Only show build validation and external status check policies in the
tree. Other policy types (minimum reviewers, required reviewers, merge
strategy) are branch policies, not CI checks, and are now filtered out
using Azure DevOps well-known policy type IDs.

Also adds statusName fallback for status policy display names.

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: show work item/comment/conflict checks, exclude only reviewer/merge-strategy policies

Switched from allow-list to deny-list for policy type filtering:
- Only excludes minimum reviewers, required reviewers, and merge strategy
- Work item linking, comment requirements, and all other policies now show
- Added merge conflict indicator from PR mergeStatus field
- Expired build evaluations stuck on "running" now show as failed
- Extracted shared computeChecksStatus helper to reduce duplication

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use allow-list for policy types and PR statuses API for build checks

Switch from deny-list to allow-list for policy type filtering (only
work item linking and comment requirements from policy evaluations).
Build/CI checks now come from the PR statuses API which always reflects
the actual build result, fixing the "stuck on running" issue.

- EXCLUDED_POLICY_TYPE_IDS → INCLUDED_POLICY_TYPE_IDS (allow-list)
- Added normalizeGuid() for case-insensitive GUID matching
- Split getChecks() into getPolicyChecks() + getBuildStatuses()
- enrichPullRequests() fetches both sources in parallel

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: revert to deny-list approach, exclude only minimum reviewers policy

Reverts the allow-list + PR statuses API approach from 1208ce4.
Goes back to the policy evaluations deny-list from 373537b, but now
only excludes the "Minimum number of reviewers" policy type.
Required reviewers, merge strategy, and all other checks remain visible.
GUID normalization retained for reliable matching.

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: resolve Required Reviewers policy to actual team/person names

For Required Reviewers policy evaluations, cross-reference the
requiredReviewerIds from the policy settings against the PR's
reviewers array to show the actual reviewer/team display name
instead of the generic "Required reviewers" label. Falls back
to the generic name when reviewer IDs can't be resolved.

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve Required Reviewers names via Azure DevOps Identities API

The previous approach cross-referenced requiredReviewerIds from policy
settings against the PR's reviewers array, but the IDs don't match
between these two sources. Now uses the Azure DevOps Identities API
(vssps.dev.azure.com) to resolve identity IDs to display names in a
single batch call. Falls back to generic label if resolution fails.

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve Required Reviewers names via PR reviewers cross-reference + Identities API fallback

Three root causes:
1. URL encoding bug: encodeURIComponent encoded commas to %2C, breaking
   the Azure DevOps Identities API which expects literal commas
2. Single source fragility: now uses PR reviewers as primary name source
   (zero extra API calls) with Identities API as fallback
3. Inconsistent GUID normalization: all ID lookups now use normalizeGuid()
   on both map keys and lookup keys

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: multi-strategy identity resolution for Required Reviewers names

Three fixes to the resolveIdentityNames function:

1. Bug fix: individual VSSPS lookups now always run for unresolved IDs,
   not only when the batch call throws. Previously if the batch returned
   HTTP 200 with empty results, individual fallback was skipped.

2. API version upgrade: changed from api-version=6.0 to 7.1-preview.1
   for the Identities endpoint, added queryMembership=None parameter.

3. New Graph API fallback: if VSSPS Identities API fails to resolve,
   the Graph Descriptors API converts the storage key to a subject
   descriptor, then the Subject Lookup API resolves display names.

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* feat: Cycle 24 P0/P0.5 - Fulfillment RabbitMQ transport, delivery failure cascade, Returns saga prerequisites, and ShippingAddress consolidation Phase A

- Fulfillment.Api/Program.cs: Add RabbitMQ transport, listen on 'fulfillment-requests', publish
  ShipmentDispatched/ShipmentDelivered/ShipmentDeliveryFailed to 'orders-fulfillment-events'
  and 'storefront-fulfillment-events' queues
- DispatchShipment.cs: Change handler return from ShipmentDispatched to (Events, OutgoingMessages)
  to emit both domain event and integration message
- ConfirmDelivery.cs: Change handler return from ShipmentDelivered to (Events, OutgoingMessages)

P0.5: Fulfillment P1 sweep
- Shipment.cs: Use Guid.Empty in Create() - Marten populates Id from stream key
- ShipmentStatus.cs: Remove dead Picking and Packing enum values (no events produce them)

commit.message:
Cross-functional priority review: defer Admin Portal, prioritize Fulfillment integrity + Returns (#340)

* Initial plan

* docs: UX Engineer review of post-Cycle 23 priority document + patch false email promise

- Complete Part II (UX Engineer Review) of priority-review-post-cycle-23.md
  - Endorse PO priority sequence (P0 bugs → Returns → Notifications → Promotions → Admin Portal)
  - Surface the false email promise in OrderConfirmation.razor as an immediate trust-debt issue
  - Provide UX requirements for the self-service returns flow:
    return window countdown, restocking fee disclosure timing, auto-approval as trust signal,
    plain-language denials, and the CS agent approval gap (no Admin Portal = no approval UI)
  - Define priority stack within Notifications Phase 1 (order confirmation → shipping → return approval)
  - Agree with Admin Portal deferral; flag risk of losing institutional memory of UX research
  - Identify OrderHistory.razor as a prerequisite to Returns (currently a placeholder)
  - Flag non-returnable category discoverability as a cross-priority dependency

- Patch OrderConfirmation.razor: remove false 'You'll receive an order confirmation email shortly'
  bullet from What's Next checklist. No Notifications BC exists; the promise was never fulfilled.
  Replace with honest copy about in-page shipment tracking.

* docs: add Part III — Principal Software Architect review to post-Cycle 23 priority document

Completes the three-part cross-functional priority review at
docs/planning/priority-review-post-cycle-23.md.

Key architectural findings:
- Reclassifies RabbitMQ transport omission from P2 to P0.5; the Fulfillment P0
  delivery-failure cascade fix is untestable in multi-process Docker Compose without
  it — both must ship in the same atomic cycle.
- Identifies critical Orders saga prerequisite gap for Returns: Handle(ReturnWindowExpired)
  unconditionally calls MarkCompleted(), which will close the saga before
  Returns.ReturnCompleted arrives and permanently block the refund path.
  Five targeted saga changes are specified as prerequisites before Returns BC ships.
- Documents ShippingAddress consolidation as a two-phase migration (dual-read JSON
  annotations in Phase A; cleanup in Phase B) to avoid null address deserialization
  of in-flight at-least-once delivered messages.
- Recommends RBAC ADR authored during Admin Portal deferral period to avoid
  retrofitting authorization onto every interim BC at Admin Portal build time.
- Confirms Product Catalog variants (P5) is a decision bottleneck (7/10 decisions
  outstanding), not a technical bottleneck — do not move up in priority.
- Adds message contract versioning ADR as a cross-cutting concern to establish
  before Returns and Notifications introduce new integration contracts.

* docs: complete cross-functional priority review — PO + UX + Principal Architect + CURRENT-CYCLE update

commit.message:
feat: provide boundary support for agent modules (#780)

## Description
Enable any agent module to run its AI agent inside Coder's Agent
Boundaries.
The agentapi module handles boundary installation, config setup, and
wrapper
script creation, then exports AGENTAPI_BOUNDARY_PREFIX for consuming
modules
to use in their start scripts.

Supports three boundary installation modes:
- coder boundary subcommand (default, Coder v2.30+)
- Standalone binary via install script (use_boundary_directly)
- Compiled from source (compile_boundary_from_source)

Users must provide a boundary config.yaml with their allowlist and
settings when enabling boundary.

Closes #457

addresses from when the quotation was created. If the company's
addresses were updated since, the card displayed stale data.

After loading the live company, compare snapshot address fields against
the current company addresses. If differences are found, update the
display refs and trigger unsaved changes so the next save re-snapshots
the updated addresses.

Only applies in draft mode — sent quotations keep their locked snapshots.

re-snapshots all updated content.

live company data, auto-save immediately so the backend re-snapshots
the current content. This prevents sending a quotation with stale
address, owner, buyer, or company data.

commit.message:
Add background notification for new PR comments (#18)

* Initial plan

* Add notification when new comments are detected on pull requests

Track unresolved comment counts across refresh cycles and show a VS Code
information message when the count increases on any pull request. The
first fetch initializes the baseline without triggering notifications.

- Single PR: "New comment on PR #123: PR Title"
- Multiple PRs: "New comments on N pull requests"

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: use 'New unresolved comments' phrasing for accuracy

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move comment notification polling to background timer

The periodic interval now calls pollForNewComments() which independently
fetches PR data and checks for new comments, regardless of whether the
sidebar panel is visible. Previously notifications only fired inside
getChildren() which VS Code skips when the tree view is not visible.

Also extracts shared fetchPullRequests() method to reduce duplication
between pollForNewComments() and getChildren().

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix notifications not showing: restore checkForNewComments in getChildren and add initial poll on activation

The previous refactor removed checkForNewComments from getChildren(),
so the baseline comment counts were only established on the first
polling interval (5 min). This meant notifications couldn't appear
until the second interval (10 min), making it seem like they were
broken.

Fix by:
1. Restoring checkForNewComments() in getChildren() for immediate
   baseline when sidebar is visible
2. Adding initial pollForNewComments() call on activation for
   immediate baseline when sidebar is not visible

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Change default polling interval from 300s to 60s

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add changelog entry for background PR comment notifications

Co-authored-by: alexanderwuytsIS <130978619+alexanderwuytsIS@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add assigned work items picker to PR creation flow (#17)

* Initial plan

* Add assigned work items picker during PR creation

- Add WorkItem interface and getAssignedWorkItems() API function using WIQL
  query to fetch non-done work items assigned to current user, then batch-fetch
  their details (id, title, state, type) in groups of up to 200
- Add azureDevops.showAssignedWorkItems setting (boolean, default true) to
  package.json so users can opt out of the picker
- During createPullRequest(), after the draft selection, show a canPickMany
  QuickPick of assigned work items; pre-select any item whose ID was parsed from
  the current branch name; use the selected set for both PR linking and work
  item state transitions
- Cache workItemProject to avoid redundant getWorkItemProject() calls

commit.message:
fix: test isolation via OLY_STATE_DIR + add 10 PTY e2e tests

- Add OLY_STATE_DIR env var to resolve_state_dir() for direct state
  directory override, bypassing platform-specific env hacks
- Fix cli_errors.rs: add OLY_SOCKET_NAME to prevent accidental
  connection to user's real daemon via default named pipe
- Update e2e_daemon.rs: use OLY_STATE_DIR instead of LOCALAPPDATA/
  XDG_STATE_HOME/HOME for reliable test isolation
- Add 10 new PTY e2e tests:
  - logs contain no CPR/DSR escape artifacts
  - multiple concurrent sessions are independent
  - session status transitions visible in oly ls
  - high-bandwidth output preserves log integrity
  - logs --keep-color preserves ANSI codes
  - logs --wait-for-prompt returns after command exits
  - stopped session logs persist on disk after eviction
  - foreground start streams full output
  - input to non-existent session fails gracefully
  - empty session list shows hint
- Update ARCHITECTURE_PTY.md: document DSR query stripping and
  persist filter in escape sequence pipeline section

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
